canonical_name: the old priestess who had schooled her
the land of the fairy folk
the elf-arrow which comes out of darkness and strikes unseen
those who hurry to fetch and carry, and hang trembling on her every word
a world where men do not come and go at a woman's beckoning
a stem Goddess whose every breath bids men live and die at her will
a petitioner to her underworld shrine
the austere dwelling of the priestesses
the Druid who gave me that name
a world where men do not wait for a woman's bidding to go and come
the young priests who are not under the silence
the younger priestesses for whom the missing of such a service was a matter for penances and guilt
a few of the younger women
any mother save the I Goddess
any woman I have ever known
a real woman there whom I could touch
the steeper, rockier part of the slope
one who knows your true name
a low stone tower which bore a crude painting of an angel
